title: Pharmacokinetic Dynamic PET (PK-PET)
sub_domain: Quantitative tracer kinetic imaging (multi-physics joint inverse)
source_data_quality_note: v3 standalone multi-physics genesis Principle. Joint forward operator couples positron-photon transport physics (annihilation, line-of-response projection, attenuation correction) with chemical compartmental ODE kinetics (tracer compartment dynamics with rate constants K_1, k_2, k_3, k_4). Recovers spatially-resolved kinetic parameter maps (K_1, k_2, k_3, k_4)(r) from time-resolved sinogram measurements y(s, t) given the plasma input function C_p(t). Distinct from L1-033 PET (static activity reconstruction) by primitive set, solution_space (kinetic parameter map vs activity map), and forward operator scope (4D dynamic vs 3D static). Authored under the standalone-as-default v3 design (see pwm-team/pwm_product/genesis/PWM_V3_STANDALONE_VS_COMPOSITE.md).
related_principles: L1-033 (PET — static activity reconstruction component, informational)
L1-034 (SPECT — sibling nuclear-medicine projection physics, informational)

E.description: Pharmacokinetic Dynamic PET (PK-PET): joint multi-physics forward couples (i) compartmental ODE tracer kinetics describing time-evolution of free and bound tracer concentrations C_T(r, t), C_T_bound(r, t) under plasma input C_p(t) and rate constants (K_1, k_2, k_3, k_4)(r); (ii) tissue activity distribution A(r, t) = alpha(r) * [C_T(r, t) + C_T_bound(r, t)] coupling chemistry to photon physics; (iii) PET projection forward y(s, t) = integral A(r, t) * h_PET(s; r) dr where h_PET is the line-of-response system matrix including attenuation, scatter, normalization, and detector response. The forward DAG has 8 primitives with two coupling constraints (n_c = 2): (i) ODE kinetics state -> tissue activity (deterministic, multi-compartment); (ii) time-resolved activity -> time-resolved projection (linear in activity, time-coupled across frames). Recovery is posed as the joint 4D inverse problem that recovers (K_1, k_2, k_3, k_4)(r) directly from y(s, t) (direct kinetic reconstruction) or via a two-step pipeline (4D-EM-ML for activity then voxelwise kinetic fitting). Standard tracers: FDG (irreversible 2-tissue with k_4=0 for metabolic rate K_i), FLT (proliferation), 11C-PiB (amyloid), 18F-FDOPA (dopamine synthesis), [18F]-Florbetapir (amyloid). Difficulty tier delta = 5 with raw condition number kappa ~ 200 (limited by count statistics and identifiability of multi-compartment model) and effective kappa_eff ~ 25 after temporal regularization and direct kinetic ML reconstruction. Mismatch parameters: input_function_uncertainty, attenuation_correction_error, scatter_correction_error, motion_during_scan, partial_volume_effect, count_statistics_dropoff. Poisson noise dominates count statistics; Gaussian thermal/electronic noise sets a smaller residual floor. See forward_model field for the closed-form joint imaging equation.
E.forward_model: dC_T/dt = K_1*C_p(t) - (k_2 + k_3)*C_T + k_4*C_T_bound; dC_T_bound/dt = k_3*C_T - k_4*C_T_bound; A(r, t) = alpha(r)*(C_T(r, t) + C_T_bound(r, t)); y(s, t) = integral A(r, t)*h_PET(s; r) dr + n_Poisson(s, t); jointly invert y(s, t) -> (K_1, k_2, k_3, k_4)(r) given C_p(t), h_PET, alpha
E.world_state_x: Spatially-resolved kinetic parameter map: (K_1, k_2, k_3, k_4)(r) with units (mL/min/g, 1/min, 1/min, 1/min). For irreversible tracers (FDG): K_i = K_1 * k_3 / (k_2 + k_3) [mL/min/g] is the metabolic rate of glucose. For reversible tracers: distribution volume V_T = K_1/k_2 * (1 + k_3/k_4) [mL/g].
E.observation_y: 4D time-resolved sinogram y(s, t) with t = N_frames temporal samples (typically 25-35 frames over 60-90 min) and s = (r, theta, axial) line-of-response coordinates. Plasma input C_p(t) is calibrated input from arterial blood sampling or image-derived from carotid artery / aorta ROI.

G.dag: L.tracer_injection -> L.compartmental_ode -> L.activity_distribution -> L.attenuation_correction -> L.scatter_correction -> L.lor_projection -> int.temporal -> int.spatial
coupling_constraints: kinetics_to_activity: A(r, t) = alpha * (C_T + C_T_bound) — deterministic coupling between ODE compartment state and tissue radioactivity at each time t; multi-compartment models introduce identifiability constraints between (K_1, k_2, k_3, k_4)

activity_to_projection: y(s, t) = integral A(r, t) * h_PET(s; r) dr — linear in activity at each time t but time-coupled across frames through ODE temporal dynamics; Poisson statistics couple count rate to noise variance

W.regime: Existence of recovered 4D kinetic parameter maps (K_1, k_2, k_3, k_4)(r) is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds for irreversible 2-tissue compartmental models (FDG-like, k_4 = 0) with sufficient temporal sampling and known C_p(t); reversible 2-tissue models (4 free parameters) require either reference-region constraint or sufficient SNR for full identifiability. Stability is moderately conditioned (kappa_eff ~ 25 after 4D-EM-ML or direct kinetic reconstruction) — input_function_uncertainty dominates K_1 bias; partial_volume_effect dominates small-structure quantitation; count_statistics_dropoff dominates late-frame variance. Joint Hadamard well-posedness for the coupled compartmental-PET forward is established by Carson (1996, 2003), Gunn-Gunn-Cunningham (2001), Patlak-Blasberg-Fenstermacher (1983 Patlak plot), Logan et al. (1990 Logan plot), Wang-Qi (2013 direct kinetic estimation), and Reader-Verhaeghe (2014 4D image reconstruction).

C.solver_class: linear-operator + convex optimisation [direct kinetic ML, 4D-EM-ML, NEGML; nested ML for kinetics + activity] | graphical analysis [Patlak plot for irreversible tracers, Logan plot for reversible tracers] | linear-operator + deep neural prior [DeepKinet, DeepPET]
C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: ||k_hat - k||_2 <= C1 * sigma_count_rate / sqrt(N_frames * total_counts). Compartmental-model-induced bias adds + C2 * |input_function_uncertainty| + C3 * |attenuation_correction_error| + C4 * |partial_volume_effect| + C5 * ||k - k_s||_1 (best s-sparse / piecewise-smooth approximation residual). Direct kinetic ML achieves q=2 (O(1/k^2)) under temporal regularization; graphical methods (Patlak, Logan) achieve q=1 linear convergence with model-bias floor. Deep-prior variants achieve faster initial convergence with bounded systematic bias. epsilon_fn captures monotone quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(H * W * Z * N_frames * (LOR_count)^(2/3)) per iteration via OS-EM with kinetic basis; direct kinetic ML O(H W Z N_frames N_compartment_params) per iteration; learned variants O(H W Z N_frames * F_theta_cost) per forward pass

joint_well_posedness_references: Patlak CS, Blasberg RG, Fenstermacher JD (1983) Graphical evaluation of blood-to-brain transfer constants from multiple-time uptake data. J Cereb Blood Flow Metab 3:1-7.

Logan J, Fowler JS, Volkow ND, Wolf AP, Dewey SL, Schlyer DJ, MacGregor RR, Hitzemann R, Bendriem B, Gatley SJ, Christman DR (1990) Graphical analysis of reversible radioligand binding from time-activity measurements applied to [N-11C-methyl]-(-)-cocaine PET studies. J Cereb Blood Flow Metab 10:740-747.
Carson RE (1996) Tracer kinetic modeling in PET. In: Positron Emission Tomography (Bailey/Townsend/Valk/Maisey eds) Springer.
Gunn RN, Gunn SR, Cunningham VJ (2001) Positron emission tomography compartmental models. J Cereb Blood Flow Metab 21(6):635-652.
Innis RB et al. (2007) Consensus nomenclature for in vivo imaging of reversibly binding radioligands. J Cereb Blood Flow Metab 27(9):1533-1539.
Wang G, Qi J (2013) Direct estimation of kinetic parametric images for dynamic PET. Theranostics 3(10):802-815.
Reader AJ, Verhaeghe J (2014) 4D image reconstruction for emission tomography. Phys Med Biol 59:R371-R418.
Tsoumpas C et al. (2008) A survey of approaches for direct parametric image reconstruction in emission tomography. Med Phys 35(9):3963-3971.

distinctness_audit.rationale: L1-033 PET reconstructs static 3D activity distribution A(r) from a single-time-frame sinogram; sensing_mechanism = annihilation_pair_detection; problem_class = linear_inverse; solution_space = 3D activity. L1-505 PK-PET reconstructs 4D kinetic parameter maps (K_1, k_2, k_3, k_4)(r) from time-resolved sinogram y(s, t) given C_p(t); sensing_mechanism = annihilation_with_compartmental_kinetics; problem_class = linear_inverse_4d; solution_space = 4D_kinetic_parameter_map. Adds L.compartmental_ode primitive and the kinetics-to-activity coupling absent from static PET. Jaccard distance over (sensing_mechanism, solution_space, problem_class, primitives) ~ 0.55 — solidly Distinct under v1 advisory thresholds (>= 0.30). The 4D temporal dimension and the recovery of kinetic rate constants (rather than instantaneous activity) make this a fundamentally different inversion target.

v3_metadata.clinical_context: Clinical research / quantitative oncology imaging. FDG-PK recovers metabolic rate of glucose (K_i = MRGlc) — gold standard for tumor staging, treatment response, and brain glucose metabolism (Alzheimer's, Parkinson's). FLT-PK recovers proliferation rate (K_1 * k_3 / (k_2 + k_3)). 11C-PiB and [18F]-Florbetapir-PK recover amyloid distribution volume V_T (Alzheimer's diagnosis). 18F-FDOPA-PK recovers presynaptic dopamine synthesis rate (Parkinson's). Standard at most academic medical centers' PET research programs. Replaces semi-quantitative SUV with absolute kinetic rates that are comparable across scans, scanners, and patients.
